component.name: Button
component.description: Triggers an action or navigation. Three visual variants (default brand-fill, outlined, ghost), three sizes, optional leading and/or trailing icon. Fully keyboard-accessible via the native button element.

when: The primary action on a page or section (use default variant, one per view)
A secondary or supplementary action alongside a primary (use outlined)
Triggering a form submit, modal open, or navigation
The action is destructive (delete, remove, deactivate) — always use the default variant so the weight signals danger; never outlined

patterns.name: form-actions
patterns.description: A primary and secondary Button side by side at the bottom of a form.

patterns.name: hero-cta
patterns.description: A single large primary Button below a Heading and Text block.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using the outlined variant for a destructive action
antiPatterns.reason: Outlined buttons read as low-priority. A destructive action (delete, remove, deactivate) needs the visual weight of the default variant so the user registers the consequence before clicking.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use variant=default. If the product needs a distinct danger colour in future, that is a new semantic token change, not a variant choice.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using Button for navigation that produces a URL
antiPatterns.reason: Produces a <button> element; links that change the URL should be <a> elements for correct semantics and accessibility.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use an anchor tag styled to match, passing className to override

antiPatterns.scenario: Placing more than one default (brand-fill) Button in the same view section
antiPatterns.reason: Multiple primary buttons compete for attention and dilute hierarchy.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use outlined for all but the single most important action

antiPatterns.scenario: Passing raw design values via the style prop
antiPatterns.reason: Breaks token alignment and prevents theme switching from working.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use the variant and size props; extend via className if needed

purpose.default: Primary action. Brand-fill background with inverted text. Use once per view section.
purpose.outlined: Secondary action. Transparent background with border. Use for supporting actions.
purpose.ghost: Inline text-link–style action. No background, no border, link text color. Use for low-emphasis actions inline with content, e.g. 'Show more' in an accordion or card.

purpose.sm: 32px height. Dense UI: table rows, inline actions, toolbars.
purpose.md: 40px height. Default for most page contexts.
purpose.lg: 48px height. Hero sections, prominent CTAs, touch-optimised flows.

purpose.square: Icon-only button with rounded-sm corners. Use in toolbars, table rows, and inline action groups. Requires the icon prop and an aria-label.
purpose.round: Icon-only button with full circular border-radius. Use for floating actions or avatar-adjacent controls. Requires the icon prop and an aria-label.

states.description: Darker background (default) or subtle fill (outlined) on pointer hover.
Muted background and text; cursor not-allowed; interaction blocked natively.

ariaAttributes: aria-label (set on icon-only buttons via shape to provide an accessible name; flows through ...rest)

keyboardInteractions.action: Moves focus to the next focusable element

accessibility.notes: Uses a native <button> element. The disabled prop sets the HTML disabled attribute, which natively prevents click events and removes the element from the tab order.
